skill.name: visual-reviewer
skill.description: Automatically review and fix visual quality issues in web pages, documents, and UI interfaces. Captures screenshots of visual output and analyzes layout, spacing, alignment, typography, color, and interactive states. Auto-fixes identified issues and re-verifies the results.
skill.summary: Automatic visual quality inspection and repair for AI-generated visual outputs
skill.author: Barry-Liu-888

security_audit.summary: The visual-reviewer skill captures screenshots of user screens and enumerates running processes for window targeting. After evaluating all 64 static findings: all are FALSE POSITIVES. The external_commands patterns are documentation code examples in SKILL.md and legitimate screenshot scripts. The weak cryptographic algorithm flags are misidentifications of comments and Chinese text. The high entropy flags are caused by bilingual Chinese/English content. The skill has a legitimate purpose of visual quality review with no evidence of data exfiltration or malicious intent. However, screen capture and process enumeration are inherently sensitive operations that require user awareness.

medium_findings.title: Screen capture capability via Python script
medium_findings.description: The skill executes a Python script that captures screenshots of windows, active windows, screen regions, or full screen. This is the core functionality of the skill and is necessary for visual review. Screenshots are saved locally to disk and analyzed by the AI. No exfiltration mechanism was found.

medium_findings.title: Window and process enumeration
medium_findings.description: The skill enumerates visible windows and running processes to target specific application windows for screenshots. This is needed to capture the correct window by title or process name.

medium_findings.title: Auto-trigger instructions in skill definition
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md contains instructions for the AI to auto-trigger after any visual output is produced. While this is common for workflow automation skills, it instructs the AI to act without explicit user request. The actions taken (screenshot and visual analysis) match the stated purpose.

low_findings.title: High entropy in bilingual markdown files
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged high entropy (6.26-6.78 bits) in SKILL.md and visual_checklist.md. This is caused by the bilingual Chinese and English text content, not by obfuscation or encoded payloads.

dangerous_patterns.title: Localhost URL for preview server
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill starts a local HTTP server on port 8765 to preview HTML files before taking screenshots. This uses localhost only and does not connect to external servers.

content.user_title: Review and fix visual quality issues in your UI outputs
content.value_statement: Visual outputs from AI often have layout, spacing, and alignment problems that are hard to catch manually. This skill automatically captures screenshots of your results, analyzes them for visual defects, and fixes the issues it finds.
actual_capabilities: Capture screenshots of specific application windows by title or process name
Analyze layout alignment, spacing consistency, and element overlap
Check typography hierarchy, line height, and text overflow
Evaluate color contrast ratios against WCAG AA standards
Detect responsive layout issues at different breakpoints
Auto-fix identified visual issues and re-verify with new screenshots
limitations: Works only on Windows operating systems (requires Win32 API)
Cannot evaluate animations or interactive transitions from static screenshots
Screenshot quality depends on screen resolution and DPI settings
Requires Python with mss and pygetwindow libraries installed

use_cases.title: Web Application UI Review
use_cases.description: After generating a web page, automatically take a screenshot of the browser window and check for layout problems, spacing issues, and alignment errors

use_cases.title: Document Format Verification
use_cases.description: Review generated documents and reports for consistent formatting, proper table alignment, and visual polish before sharing with stakeholders

use_cases.title: Design Implementation Check
use_cases.description: Verify that UI implementations match design specifications by analyzing screenshots for correct spacing, colors, and typography

prompt_templates.title: Quick layout check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a visual review on the page I just created. Check for alignment issues, overlapping elements, and spacing consistency.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a basic layout inspection

prompt_templates.title: Full quality audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a complete visual review using the checklist in references/visual_checklist.md. Score each dimension from 0 to 3 and create a report with screenshots for each finding.

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the color contrast of all text elements in my page. Verify that body text meets WCAG AA 4.5:1 ratio and large text meets 3:1. Report any failures.

prompt_templates.title: Responsive review with fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my page at 768px, 1024px, and 1440px widths. For each breakpoint, capture a screenshot, identify layout issues, fix the CSS, and re-verify with a new screenshot. Output a before and after comparison.

output_examples.input: Check the layout of my dashboard page
output: 1. [Medium] Chart container overflow on right side - fixed with overflow: hidden
2. [Minor] Button group alignment off by 4px - fixed with flex alignment
Re-verified: Issues 1 and 2 confirmed fixed. Issue 3 pending user approval.

output_examples.input: Run a full visual review on this report
output: Data Display: 2/3 - Number columns now right-aligned (fixed)
Edge Cases: 3/3 - Empty state placeholder present

output_examples.input: Check if my form is accessible
output: Button contrast: FAIL (3.1:1, needs 4.5:1) - Changed button color from #999 to #666 (4.8:1)
Focus states: FAIL - Tab navigation had no visible indicator - Added outline: 2px solid blue
Label associations: PASS - All form inputs have proper labels
Touch targets: PASS - All interactive elements are at least 44x44px

best_practices: Use targeted window screenshots with --title or --process instead of fullscreen to capture only the relevant output
Fix high-severity issues first, then re-verify before moving to minor issues
Limit fix-verify cycles to 3 iterations to avoid infinite loops
anti_patterns: Making visual changes based on code review alone without taking a screenshot first
Ignoring WCAG color contrast requirements in favor of visual preference
Adjusting layout at one breakpoint without checking other screen sizes

faq.question: Which operating systems does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill works only on Windows. The screenshot scripts use Win32 API calls and Windows-specific tools.

faq.question: What software do I need to install?
faq.answer: You need Python 3 with the mss and pygetwindow packages. The requirements.txt file lists all dependencies.

faq.question: Does this skill upload my screenshots anywhere?
faq.answer: No. Screenshots are saved to your local disk and read by the AI for analysis. No data is sent to external servers.

faq.question: Can I use this skill on mobile app screenshots?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for desktop Windows applications. Mobile screenshots would need a different capture method.

faq.question: How many visual dimensions does the review cover?
faq.answer: The review covers 7 dimensions: layout, spacing, typography, color, interaction, data display, and edge cases.

faq.question: What happens if the screenshot capture fails?
faq.answer: The script outputs debug information including a list of visible windows to help identify the correct window title.
